title: Introduction to the cerebroApp workflow (Seurat)
text: Now, create Seurat object filter cells less 50 transcripts fewer 10 expressed genes. cut-offs reasonable example data set likely need adjusted real data set. , follow standard Seurat workflow, including… log-normalization transcript counts, identification highly variably genes, scaling expression values regression number transcripts per cell, principal component analysis (PCA).
code: seurat <- CreateSeuratObject(   project = 'pbmc',   counts = pbmc,   min.cells = 5 ) seurat <- subset(seurat, subset = nCount_RNA >= 50 & nFeature_RNA >= 10) seurat <- NormalizeData(seurat, verbose = FALSE) seurat <- FindVariableFeatures(seurat, verbose = FALSE) seurat <- ScaleData(seurat, vars.to.regress = 'nCount_RNA', verbose = FALSE) seurat <- RunPCA(seurat, features = seurat@assays$RNA@var.features, verbose = FALSE)

title: Create expression matrix in h5 format
text: vignette, explain can save expression matrix data set .h5/TENxMatrix format use Cerebro. Using .h5/TENxMatrix format results low memory footprint application expression values specific genes directly read disk instead load entire matrix memory. Moreover, depending size, time takes load data set Cerebro may greatly reduced meta data tables must loaded, usually small compared expression matrix. catch: Reading data disk tends slower reading memory, always noticeable may compromise worth making.

title: Create expression matrix in h5 format
text: Make sure Matrix, HDF5Array cerebroApp packages installed. example, store output temporary directory. expression matrix look similar :
code: if ( 'Matrix' %in% installed.packages() == FALSE ) install.packages('Matrix') if ( 'HDF5Array' %in% installed.packages() == FALSE ) install.packages('HDF5Array') if ( 'cerebroApp' %in% installed.packages() == FALSE ) install.packages('cerebroApp')  library(HDF5Array) library(HDF5Array) output_dir <- tempdir() ```# Load existing `.crb` file  Assuming that you have already exported your data set to a `.crb` file using the `exportFromSeurat()` or `exportFromSCE()` functions, we are first going to load that file. Here, we use the example data set in the `cerebroApp` package: crb_input <- system.file('extdata', 'v1.4', 'example.crb', package = 'cerebroAppLite') crb <- readRDS(crb_input) str(crb$expression)

title: Create expression matrix in h5 format
text: Instead storing matrix current (conventional) format rows representing genes columns representing cells, first transpose . means, rows (genes) become columns, columns (cells) become rows. way matrix indexed greatly favors extraction columns rather rows. Cerebro, extract expression values genes, rotate matrix. Importantly, Cerebro expects find matrix expression group .h5 file, make sure specify call HDF5Array::writeTENxMatrix() function. Creating connection file easy : Looks similar matrix started .
code: writeTENxMatrix(   Matrix::t(crb$expression),   glue::glue('{output_dir}/example_expression.h5'),   group = 'expression' ) h5_output <- glue::glue('{output_dir}/example_expression.h5') expression_matrix_h5 <- TENxMatrix(h5_output, group='expression') str(expression_matrix_h5)

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: example workflow, load small transcript count table Seurat package containing 80 cells 230 genes. want try workflow real data set, can find transcript count matrices Cerebro GitHub repo.
code: pbmc_counts <- read.table(     file = system.file('extdata', 'pbmc_raw.txt', package = 'Seurat'),     as.is = TRUE   ) %>% as.matrix()

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: Now, create SingleCellExperiment object filter cells less 50 transcripts fewer 10 expressed genes. cut-offs reasonable example data set likely need adjusted real data set. , … remove genes expressed less 5 cells, log-normalize transcript counts, identify highly variably genes, perform principal component analysis (PCA).
code: sce <- SingleCellExperiment::SingleCellExperiment(   assays = list(counts = pbmc_counts) )  qc_metrics <- perCellQCMetrics(sce) colData(sce) <- cbind(colData(sce), qc_metrics)  cells_to_keep <- sce$sum >= 50 & sce$detected >= 10 sce <- sce[,cells_to_keep]  genes_to_keep <- nexprs(sce, byrow=TRUE) >= 5 sce <- sce[genes_to_keep,]  sce <- logNormCounts(sce)  variable_genes <- modelGeneVar(sce) highly_variable_genes <- getTopHVGs(variable_genes, n = 50)  sce <- runPCA(sce)

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: , identify clusters using graph-based approach.
code: graph <- buildSNNGraph(sce, use.dimred = "PCA") cluster <- igraph::cluster_walktrap(graph)$membership sce$cluster <- factor(cluster)

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: Next, use UMAP technique generate two- three-dimensional projections.
code: sce <- runUMAP(   sce,   name = 'UMAP',   ncomponents = 2, )  sce <- runUMAP(   sce,   name = 'UMAP_3D',   ncomponents = 3, )

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: example data set consists single sample just add name meta data. Moreover, order able understand analysis later, add meta data misc slot SCE object, e.g. experiment name species. info store later collected visualization Cerebro.
code: sce$sample <- factor('pbmc', levels = 'pbmc')  sce@metadata$experiment <- list(   experiment_name = 'pbmc',   organism = 'hg',   date_of_analysis = Sys.Date() )  sce@metadata$parameters <- list(   gene_nomenclature = 'gene_name',   discard_genes_expressed_in_fewer_cells_than = 10,   keep_mitochondrial_genes = TRUE,   variables_to_regress_out = 'nUMI' )  sce@metadata$parameters$filtering <- list(   UMI_min = 50,   UMI_max = Inf,   genes_min = 10,   genes_max = Inf )  sce@metadata$technical_info$cerebroApp_version <- utils::packageVersion('cerebroApp') sce@metadata$technical_info$scran <- utils::packageVersion('scran') sce@metadata$technical_info$scater <- utils::packageVersion('scater') sce@metadata$technical_info <- list(   'R' = capture.output(devtools::session_info()) )

title: Export a data set in SCE format
text: Using SingleR package, can get suggestion cell type cell. Apart suggested cell type, also extract score might help us understand confident assignment . ##Identify marker genes Marker genes genes particularly strongly weakly expressed given cell population, e.g. cluster. information can help distinguish role different cell groups data set. , marker genes identified clusters cell types using findMarkers() function scran package. results separate tables respective subgroups, merge together order make compatible Cerebro.
code: singler_ref <- BlueprintEncodeData()  singler_results_blueprintencode_main <- SingleR(   test = logcounts(sce),   ref = singler_ref,   labels = singler_ref@colData@listData$label.main )  sce$cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main <- singler_results_blueprintencode_main@listData$labels  singler_scores <- singler_results_blueprintencode_main@listData$scores %>%   as_tibble() %>%   dplyr::mutate(assigned_score = NA)  for ( i in seq_len(nrow(singler_scores)) ) {   singler_scores$assigned_score[i] <- singler_scores[[singler_results_blueprintencode_main@listData$labels[i]]][i] }  sce$cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main_score <- singler_scores$assigned_score

title: Export a data set in SCE format
code: sce@metadata$marker_genes <- list() sce@metadata$marker_genes$scran <- list()  marker_genes_cluster <- findMarkers(sce, sce$cluster)  for ( i in seq_along(marker_genes_cluster) ) {   marker_genes_cluster[[i]] <- as.data.frame(marker_genes_cluster[[i]]) %>%     mutate(       cluster = names(marker_genes_cluster)[i],       gene = rownames(.)     )   marker_genes_cluster[[i]][[paste0('logFC.', names(marker_genes_cluster)[i])]] <- 0 }  marker_genes_cluster <- do.call(bind_rows, marker_genes_cluster) %>%   select(cluster, gene, Top, p.value, FDR, everything()) %>%   mutate(cluster = factor(cluster, levels = levels(colData(sce)$cluster)))  sce@metadata$marker_genes$scran$cluster <- marker_genes_cluster %>%   filter(!is.na(Top), FDR <= 0.1)  glimpse(marker_genes_cluster)  marker_genes_cluster %>% head() %>% knitr::kable()

title: Export a data set in SCE format
code: marker_genes_cell_type <- findMarkers(sce, sce$cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main)  for ( i in seq_along(marker_genes_cell_type) ) {   marker_genes_cell_type[[i]] <- as.data.frame(marker_genes_cell_type[[i]]) %>%     mutate(       cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main = names(marker_genes_cell_type)[i],       gene = rownames(.)     )   marker_genes_cell_type[[i]][[paste0('logFC.', gsub(names(marker_genes_cell_type)[i], pattern = ' |\\-|\\+', replacement = '.'))]] <- 0 }  marker_genes_cell_type <- do.call(bind_rows, marker_genes_cell_type) %>%   select(cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main, gene, Top, p.value, FDR, everything()) %>%   mutate(cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main = factor(       cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main,       levels = unique(cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main)     )   )  sce@metadata$marker_genes$scran$cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main <- marker_genes_cell_type %>%   filter(!is.na(Top), FDR <= 0.1)  glimpse(marker_genes_cell_type)  marker_genes_cell_type %>% select(1:6) %>% head() %>% knitr::kable(booktabs = T)

title: Export and visualize custom tables and plots
text: second example, prepare dot plot showing expression significant marker gene cluster. plot ready, can store Seurat object export . Shown first screenshot interactive version plot:  notice legend dot size missing. cases plot isn’t shown correctly (conversion plotly works well always perfectly), can switch interactivity see plain, unmodified plot:
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(tidyr)  # cells will be grouped by clusters that they have been assigned to cluster_ids <- levels(seurat@meta.data$seurat_clusters)  # select a set of genes for which we want to show expression genes_to_show <- seurat@misc$marker_genes$cerebro_seurat$seurat_clusters %>%   group_by(seurat_clusters) %>%   arrange(p_val_adj) %>%   slice(1) %>%   pull(gene)  # for every cluster-gene combination, calculate the average expression across # all cells and then transform the data into a data frame expression_levels_per_cluster <- vapply(     cluster_ids, FUN.VALUE = numeric(length(cluster_ids)), function(x) {       cells_in_current_cluster <- which(seurat@meta.data$seurat_cluster == x)       Matrix::rowMeans(seurat@assays$SCT@data[genes_to_show,cells_in_current_cluster])     }   ) %>%   t() %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   mutate(cluster = rownames(.)) %>%   select(cluster, everything()) %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(2:ncol(.)),     names_to = 'gene'   ) %>%   rename(expression = value) %>%   mutate(id_to_merge = paste0(cluster, '_', gene))  # for every cluster-gene combination, calculate the percentage of cells in the # respective group that has at least 1 transcript (this means we consider it # as expressing the gene) and then transform the data into a data frame percentage_of_cells_expressing_gene <- vapply(     cluster_ids, FUN.VALUE = numeric(length(cluster_ids)), function(x) {       cells_in_current_cluster <- which(seurat@meta.data$seurat_cluster == x)       Matrix::rowSums(seurat@assays$SCT@data[genes_to_show,cells_in_current_cluster] != 0)     }   ) %>%   t() %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   mutate(cluster = rownames(.)) %>%   select(cluster, everything()) %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(2:ncol(.)),     names_to = 'gene'   ) %>%   rename(cell_count = value) %>%   left_join(     .,     seurat@meta.data %>%       group_by(seurat_clusters) %>%       tally() %>%       rename(cluster = seurat_clusters),     by = 'cluster') %>%   mutate(     id_to_merge = paste0(cluster, '_', gene),     percent_cells = cell_count / n   )  # merge the two data frames created before and plot the data custom_plot <- left_join(     expression_levels_per_cluster,     percentage_of_cells_expressing_gene %>% select(id_to_merge, percent_cells),     by = 'id_to_merge'   ) %>%   mutate(     cluster = factor(cluster, levels = rev(cluster_ids)),     gene = factor(gene, levels = genes_to_show)   ) %>%   ggplot(aes(gene, cluster)) +   geom_point(aes(color = expression, size = percent_cells)) +   scale_color_distiller(     palette = 'Reds',     direction = 1,     name = 'Log-normalised\nexpression',     guide = guide_colorbar(frame.colour = "black", ticks.colour = "black")   ) +   scale_size(name = 'Percent\nof cells', labels = scales::percent) +   labs(y = 'Cluster', color = 'Expression') +   coord_fixed() +   theme_bw() +   theme(     axis.title.x = element_blank(),     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)   ) seurat@misc$extra_material$plots <- list(   "dot_plot_marker_genes_clusters" = custom_plot )  exportFromSeurat(   seurat,   assay = 'SCT',   slot = 'data',   file = '~/Dropbox/Cerebro_development/pbmc_Seurat.crb',   experiment_name = 'pbmc_Seurat',   organism = 'hg',   groups = c('sample','seurat_clusters','cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main'),   cell_cycle = c('cell_cycle_seurat'),   nUMI = 'nCount_RNA',   nGene = 'nFeature_RNA',   add_all_meta_data = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE ) [10:03:13] Start collecting data... [10:03:14] Overview of Cerebro object:  class: Cerebro_v1.3 cerebroApp version: 1.3.0 experiment name: pbmc_Seurat organism: hg date of analysis: 2020-02-19 date of export: 2020-09-25 number of cells: 5,697 number of genes: 15,907 grouping variables (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main cell cycle variables (1): cell_cycle_seurat projections (2): UMAP, UMAP_3D trees (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main most expressed genes: sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main marker genes:   - cerebro_seurat (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main enriched pathways:   - cerebro_seurat_enrichr (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main,    - cerebro_GSVA (3): sample, seurat_clusters, cell_type_singler_blueprintencode_main trajectories:   - monocle2 (1): highly_variable_genes extra material:   - plots (1): dot_plot_marker_genes_clusters  [10:03:14] Saving Cerebro object to: ~/Dropbox/Cerebro_development/pbmc_Seurat.crb [10:03:18] Done!

title: Interactive visualization of single cell transcriptomics
text: exportFromSeurat() (convertSeuratToCerebro()) accept expression_matrix_mode = c("embedded", "bpcells", "h5") count matrix persisted alongside .crb: Benchmark trade-offs PBMC fixture (38,606 genes × 147,756 cells): Browser-side TTFB / DOM-ready / load within ~10 ms across backends — divergence second row server-side R work (readRDS + .attachExternalExpression()) plus constant ~5 s Shiny session handshake. Disk-size caveat — depends fixture size. 391 MB h5 total large fixture HDF5’s default gzip filter TENx CSC layout compresses sparse counts well (~6× vs uncompressed). small/dense fixtures trade-can flip — example Roman Hillje’s inst/extdata/v1.4/example.h5 (1000 cells × 500 genes) sibling .h5 actually larger equivalent embedded .crb , metadata + chunk overhead dominates compressed payload. h5 win RAM load time consistent across fixture sizes; win disk emerges scale. Since 1.7.0 bpcells exporter automatically calls BPCells::convert_matrix_type("uint32_t") whenever input values losslessly representable non-negative integers (typical scRNA-seq counts case), triggers BPCells’s bit-packed integer storage shrinks sibling ~5× vs raw double; normalised float values (slot = "data" / "scale.data") exporter falls back raw storage avoid silent precision loss. Picking one: h5 (recommended default) — fastest startup, lowest RAM, fastest queries; smallest disk large fixtures (subject caveat ). TENx CSC layout aligns Cerebro reads expression (per-gene = single column slice), HDF5 page-caching makes repeated reads memory-fast without committing whole matrix RAM. Requires HDF5Array Bioconductor package host. bpcells — RAM-constrained host large matrices, workloads dominated chunk-level batched operations rather per-gene reads. Disk size similar h5 integer counts (bit-packed since 1.7.0); per-gene query ~0.7 s, chunk-level batched ops benefit per-gene streaming. embedded — single-file convenience (sibling manage), compatibility old .crb readers. ~14 s end--end pins full matrix RAM per loaded copy. Best small datasets one-shot scripts. reference, 1.7.0 lazy h5 refactor, h5 attach eager (rhdf5::h5read + full dgCMatrix reconstruction), giving ~33 s open-URL time, ~11 GB RSS, ~0.45 s queries — .e. lazy-h5 backend attach ~263× faster, RAM ~10× smaller, queries ~45× faster, web load ~4× faster. createShinyApp() already knows <stem>.bpcells/ <stem>.h5 copies next bundled .crb. Shiny runtime re-resolves sibling location load via getExpressionBackend()$location relative .crb’s parent directory, bundle stays portable.

title: R6 class in which data sets will be stored for visualization in Cerebro. — Cerebro_v1.3
text: version cerebroApp version used create object. experiment list contains meta data data set, including experiment name, species, date export. technical_info list contains technical information analysis, including R session info. parameters list contains important parameters used analysis, e.g. cut-values cell filtering. groups list contains specified grouping variables group levels (subgroups) belong . grouping variable, corresponding column name must exist meta data. cell_cycle vector contains name columns meta data contain cell cycle assignments. gene_lists list contains gene lists, e.g. mitochondrial /ribosomal genes. expression matrix-like object holds transcript counts. expression_backend list describing /expression matrix stored. step 7.1 every newly exported object tags list(type = "embedded", location = NULL); future step 7.2 introduce type = "h5" / "bpcells" external location. Older .crb files (serialised field existed) load expression_backend = NULL; getExpressionBackend() treats "embedded" backward compatibility. meta_data data.frame contains cell meta data. projections list contains projections/dimensional reductions. most_expressed_genes list contains data.frame holding expressed genes grouping variable specified call getMostExpressedGenes. mean_expression list contains data.frame holding mean expression per gene grouping variable. marker_genes list contains list every method used calculate marker genes, data.frame grouping variable, e.g. specified call getMarkerGenes. enriched_pathways list contains list every method used calculate marker genes, data.frame grouping variable, e.g. specified call getEnrichedPathways performGeneSetEnrichmentAnalysis. trees list contains phylogenetic tree (class phylo) grouping variables. trajectories list contains list every method used calculate trajectories, , depending method, data.frame list specific trajectory, e.g. extracted extractMonocleTrajectory. extra_material list can contain additional material related data set; tables stored data.frame format named list called `tables` immune_repertoire list data.frames (one per sample) containing scRepertoire columns (CTgene, CTnt, CTaa, CTstrict, etc.). bcr_data list contains BCR data (kept backward compatibility older .crb files). tcr_data list contains TCR data (kept backward compatibility older .crb files).

Expression",   add_most_expressed_genes = TRUE,   most_expressed_genes = NULL,   bcr_file = NULL,   tcr_file = NULL )

title: Convert Seurat Object to Cerebro Format — convertSeuratToCerebro
text: seurat_file Character string specifying path Seurat object file. Supported format: .rds. result_dir Character string specifying directory Cerebro output file (.crb) saved. assay Character string specifying assay use Seurat object; default: "RNA". slot Character string specifying slot extract expression data (e.g., "data", "counts", "scale.data"); default: "data". experiment_name Character string experiment name stored Cerebro object; default: "Dura Mater - Cells". organism Character string specifying organism (e.g., "Human", "Mouse"); default: "Human". groups Character vector column names Seurat metadata use grouping variables; default: c("seurat_clusters", "orig.ident", "cell_type_final"). groups_naming Named list renaming grouping variables. Names old column names, values new names; default: NULL. max_group_levels Numeric value specifying maximum number unique levels allowed grouping variable. Groups unique values threshold excluded; default: 100. nUMI Character string specifying column name metadata containing number UMIs per cell; default: "nCount_RNA". nGene Character string specifying column name metadata containing number expressed genes per cell; default: "nFeature_RNA". add_all_meta_data Logical indicating whether include metadata columns export; default: TRUE. use_delayed_array Logical indicating whether convert expression data DelayedArray format memory efficiency; default: FALSE. Ignored expression_matrix_mode set external backend. expression_matrix_mode persist expression matrix generated .crb. One "embedded" (default), "bpcells" "h5". See exportFromSeurat details; briefly, "embedded" stores matrix inside .crb (legacy behaviour), "bpcells" writes -disk BPCells directory next .crb keeps lightweight handle inside (typically reduces .crb size ~80 "h5" writes TENx-format HDF5 file next .crb Shiny runtime loads lazily, minimising RAM startup time (recommended default large datasets). Shiny runtime re-resolves backends relative .crb's parent directory, packaging .crb sibling <stem>.bpcells/ <stem>.h5 together enough portable deployment. verbose Logical indicating whether print progress messages; default: TRUE. cell_cycle Character vector column names metadata containing cell cycle phase assignments; default: NULL. marker_file Character string specifying path marker gene table file. Supported formats: .csv, .tsv, .txt, .tab; default: NULL. marker_method Character string specifying name method used identify marker genes (used label Cerebro); default: "Diff. Expression". add_most_expressed_genes Logical indicating whether calculate expressed genes group; default: TRUE. most_expressed_genes Optional pre-calculated expressed genes data. Can either data.frame (converted list(unknown = ...)) list data.frames. list elements unnamed, assigned names like "unknown1", "unknown2", etc.; default: NULL. bcr_file Character string specifying path BCR data file (.rds format). data merged unified immune_repertoire slot Seurat object export; default: NULL. tcr_file Character string specifying path TCR data file (.rds format). data merged unified immune_repertoire slot Seurat object export; default: NULL.

text: function return value. saves Cerebro object (.crb file) specified result_dir.

title: Convert Seurat Object to Cerebro Format — convertSeuratToCerebro
text: function performs following steps: Reads Seurat object seurat_file Renames grouping columns groups_naming provided Loads marker gene tables marker_file provided: Excel files multiple sheets, sheet becomes         separate group single-sheet files, data split first column Exports processed data using exportFromSeurat Saves result cerebro_<basename>.crb result_dir Cleans memory removing Seurat object calling garbage     collection

text: object Seurat object. assay Assay pull expression values ; defaults RNA. slot Slot pull expression values ; defaults data. recommended use sparse data (log-transformed raw counts) instead dense data (scaled slot) avoid performance bottlenecks Cerebro interface. file save output. experiment_name Experiment name. organism Organism, e.g. hg (human), mm (mouse), etc. groups Names grouping variables meta data (object@meta.data), e.g. c("sample","cluster"); least one must provided; defaults NULL. main_group primary grouping variable use display Cerebro; must one grouping variables specified groups; defaults NULL. cell_cycle Names columns meta data (object@meta.data) contain cell cycle information, e.g. c("Phase"); defaults NULL. nUMI Column object@meta.data contains information number transcripts per cell; defaults nUMI. nGene Column object@meta.data contains information number expressed genes per cell; defaults nGene. add_all_meta_data set TRUE, meta data columns extracted well. use_delayed_array set TRUE, expression matrix stored RleMatrix (see DelayedArray package). can useful large data sets, matrix loaded memory instead values read disk directly, cost performance. Note necessary install DelayedArray package. set FALSE (default), expression matrix copied input object . recommended use sparse format, dgCMatrix Matrix package. Ignored expression_matrix_mode set external backend. expression_matrix_mode persist expression matrix. One "embedded" (default), "bpcells", "h5". "embedded" stores matrix inside .crb file,   . Compatible existing .crb readers. "bpcells" writes matrix BPCells -disk directory   next .crb keeps lightweight handle   serialised object. Recommended large sparse matrices. resulting   .crb portable long sibling .bpcells/ directory   travels ; Shiny runtime re-resolves paths via   getExpressionBackend()$location relative .crb's   parent directory (step 7.3 runtime attach). "h5" writes matrix via HDF5Array::writeTENxMatrix()   TENx-format sparse HDF5 file next .crb (sibling   <stem>.h5) tags backend relative location.   -disk layout matches inst/extdata/v1.4/example.h5: single   /expression group data, indices, indptr,   shape, genes, barcodes datasets. matrix   stored cells x genes (TENx column-favoured, optimised per-gene   reads); Shiny runtime attach reads back lazy   HDF5Array::TENxMatrix seed transposes lazily Cerebro's   internal genes x cells layout via DelayedArray::t() (free).   -memory dgCMatrix never materialised attach, RAM stays   close .crb metadata size. Requires HDF5Array   package. verbose Set TRUE want additional log messages; defaults FALSE.

title: cerebroAppLite 1.7.0
text: External HDF5 expression backend, symmetric bpcells backend: exportFromSeurat() expression_matrix_mode = "h5" writes matrix via HDF5Array::writeTENxMatrix() TENx-format .h5 next .crb. runtime attach loads back lazy HDF5Array::TENxMatrix seed transposes via DelayedArray::t() (free); -memory dgCMatrix never materialised, RAM stays close .crb metadata size attach effectively instant Introduced createShinyApp() bundling self-contained Shiny app one .crb files createShinyApp() now copies <stem>.h5 sibling alongside .crb app bundling, mirroring existing .bpcells/ handling Legacy .crb files (predating expression_backend field) auto-tagged h5 host app sets Cerebro.options[["expression_matrix_h5"]], finally giving inst/extdata/v1.4/example.h5 runtime consumer convertSeuratToCerebro() accepts -memory Seurat object alongside .rds path; output basename derives experiment_name path given createShinyApp() opens ... passthrough callers can forward extra options without signature churn exportFromSeurat() expression_matrix_mode = "bpcells" now auto-detects losslessly-integer values calls BPCells::convert_matrix_type("uint32_t") write_matrix_dir(), triggers BPCells’s bit-packed integer storage typical scRNA-seq counts case. Shrinks bpcells sibling ~5× integer counts (e.g. 50k cells × 20k genes: 440 MB raw double → 78 MB bit-packed; PBMC Samples 38,606 × 147,756: 2.6 GB → 549 MB), queries get ~1.5-1.7× faster side effect (smaller payload read). Normalised float values (slot = "data"/"scale.data") fall back raw double avoid silent precision loss
